title: UNESCO/Japan Young Researchers' Fellowships Programme (Keizo Obuchi Fellowships)
description: Supports outstanding young researchers undertaking postgraduate research at a Japanese university or research institution, in areas contributing to sustainable development and international cooperation. The 2026 call focuses on heritage conservation and management, sustainable development, and artificial intelligence, with attention to interdisciplinary research connecting these themes to social, ethical, and governance dimensions. Ten fellowships of up to $10,000 USD each support a research stay of 3 to 9 months in Japan. Applicants must be 40 or under, hold at least a master's degree, and secure acceptance from a host supervisor at a Japanese university or research institution before applying. Eligible applicants come from Africa, Small Island Developing States, Ukraine, and Türkiye, including named Global Partnership for Education Knowledge and Innovation Exchange (GPE KIX) partner countries: Ukraine, Maldives, Timor-Leste, Fiji, Kiribati, the Federated States of Micronesia, Papua New Guinea, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, and Vanuatu.
visaNote: Researchers travel to Japan on a student/researcher visa arranged with the host institution's support; this is a research fellowship, not employment.

title: WIPO (World Intellectual Property Organization) Internship Programme
description: A paid internship at WIPO headquarters in Geneva, open to current final-year undergraduates as well as graduates who completed their degree within the past two years, in fields including intellectual property law, economics, technology, communications, translation, project management, and administration. Placements run roughly 2 to 12 months depending on the hosting division's needs. WIPO covers round-trip airfare to Geneva, provides visa assistance, and pays a monthly stipend of CHF 1,645 (undergraduate) or CHF 2,170 (graduate), plus local transportation support -- unlike several other UN-agency internships that only offer a modest local-expenses stipend with no travel coverage. Applications go through WIPO's Internship Roster, which is not tied to a specific position or single deadline: applicants apply anytime, their profile stays active for one year, and it is matched against openings as they arise across the organization.
visaNote: WIPO provides visa assistance for interns relocating to Geneva, Switzerland.
